title: AI, ML & Data Engineering
gist: This is an InfoQ topic index/listing page for the AI, ML & Data Engineering section, not a substantive article — it enumerates recent article, news, presentation, podcast, and guide headlines, whose dominant theme is securing AI agents and the maturation of agentic systems.

insights.claim: InfoQ's AI, ML & Data Engineering section as of June 2026 is dominated by AI-security and agent-governance content, including articles on ML model poisoning, governing AI in the cloud, AI-driven phishing, and a 'Securing the AI Stack: from Model to Production' series.
insights.speaker: InfoQ
insights.so_what: Editorial concentration on AI security signals where practitioner attention and tooling demand are clustering in mid-2026.
insights.locator: Articles about AI, ML & Data Engineering

insights.claim: The page's presentation and podcast listings center on agentic systems maturation — 'From Hype to Strong Foundations: What the Rise, Fall and Resurgence of Agents Can Teach Us', context engineering and memory management, and operating models for autonomous agents — indicating agents have moved from hype to production-discipline discussion.
insights.speaker: InfoQ
insights.so_what: A shift in conference/podcast framing from agent hype to operational foundations marks the technology entering a maturity phase.
insights.quote: From Hype to Strong Foundations: What the Rise, Fall and Resurgence of Agents Can Teach Us about Outlasting the Cycle
insights.locator: Presentations about AI, ML & Data Engineering
